description_long: This object is a rectangular cardboard medicine box, predominantly white with teal and blue accents. The front and sides are densely covered with printed text, including the medicine name 'Ventolin', dosage information, and manufacturer details. The box features a barcode, a QR code, and Braille dots for accessibility. The GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) logo is visible on one side, and the packaging appears to be sealed and intact. The overall finish is matte, with a smooth, hard surface typical of pharmaceutical cartons. The box is likely to contain a pressurized inhaler or similar medical device inside.
description: A white and teal rectangular medicine box labeled 'Ventolin' with detailed text, Braille, and a GSK logo, likely containing an inhaler.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the box with the barcode, QR code, and manufacturer address, along with some Braille and the GSK logo at the bottom.
description_view_1: This view displays the main label with the medicine name 'Ventolin', dosage, and instructions in Spanish, as well as Braille text.
description_view_2: This view highlights the top and side of the box, showing the product name, dosage, and a continuation of the Braille and printed information.
description_view_3: This view presents the front of the box with the GSK logo, main product information, and Braille, making it the most informative side for identification.
